expectations.evidence: Field extracted as 'internal static readonly' rather than 'private static readonly' — broader visibility than needed.

expectations.text: No files outside Gen.cs were modified
expectations.evidence: Agent also modified StringStrategy.cs to use Gen.PrintableAscii — scope violation.

notes: Scope discipline is the key differentiator here. Without skill guidance, agent modified both files (reasonable, but violates the single-file constraint).
Assertion 'dotnet test src/ passes' is the primary discriminator: 3/3 with_skill runs ran the full suite; 0/3 without_skill runs did. This mirrors the implement skill pattern.
comment-cleanup is the only eval where without_skill outscored with_skill (0.75 vs 0.50) — the skill's 3-agent structure caused over-reach beyond the comment-only task.
Scope enforcement (no files outside X modified) is the second key discriminator: with_skill 3/3 pass; without_skill 1/3 pass.
with_skill runs cost ~31.6s more and ~3653 more tokens on average — the overhead of running 3 parallel review agents.
hot-path-efficiency/without_skill appears to have re-implemented ListStrategy rather than simplifying an existing one — possibly worktree issue or misread of the prompt.
